commentary_text: The market assigns Belgium a 67% win probability versus the model's 38%, a 29-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The model sees Iran as competitive, pricing them at 30%, while the market credits them at just 12%—a 18-point spread suggesting real-money flows are pricing in information the model cannot access. Recent form, injuries, or lineup changes could all sharpen conviction in Belgium's superiority; the dashboard surfaces this as a meaningful signal without adjudicating which source has better visibility into such factors.
